zh.Eastern Division, Marshall, Alabama, Uni: 美國阿拉巴馬州沃克易亞斯特 迪維希
zh.lifeSummary: 當Nancy Young於大約1800年出生在美國南卡羅來納州時，她的父親Isaac Young IV42歲，母親MARY Odingsell42歲。她和John Allen Griffith至少有2個兒子和4個女兒。她於1850年住在美國喬治亞戈登且於1860年住在美國阿拉巴馬州沃克易亞斯特 迪維希。她去世於1870年6月18日之前在美國阿拉巴馬州。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Nancy Young于大约1800年出生在美国南卡罗来纳州时，她的父亲Isaac Young IV42岁，母亲MARY Odingsell42岁。她和John Allen Griffith至少有2个儿子和4个女儿。她于1850年住在美国乔治亚州戈登且于1860年住在美国亚拉巴马州沃克易亞斯特 迪維希。她去世于1870年6月18日之前在美国亚拉巴马州。

nameMeanings.name: Young
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and northern Irish: nickname from Middle English yong ‘young’ (Old English geong), used to distinguish a younger man from an older man bearing the same personal name (typically, father and son). In Middle English this name is often found with the Anglo-Norman French definite article, for example Robert le Yunge. In Gaelic-speaking areas of Scotland this was widely used as an English equivalent of the Gaelic nickname Og ‘young’; see Ogg . This surname is also very common among African Americans.

Americanized form (translation into English) of various European surnames meaning ‘young’ or similar, notably German   Jung  , Dutch   Jong   and   De Jong  , and French   Lejeune   and   Lajeunesse  .
Americanized form of Swedish Ljung: topographic or an ornamental name from ljung ‘(field of) heather’, or a habitational name from a placename containing this word, e.g. Ljungby.

variants.name: Yongue
meanings: English: variant of   Young  . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.
Perhaps also an Americanized form of Dutch   De Jonge  .

variants.name: Youngs
meanings: English (Norfolk): variant of Young , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Younger
meanings: English (mainly Northumberland and eastern Scotland): from Middle English yungre ‘younger’, comparative of Young , used to distinguish a younger man from an older man bearing the same personal name (typically, father and son). In one case, at least, however, the name is known to have been borne by an immigrant Fleming, and was probably an Americanized form of Middle Dutch jongheer ‘young nobleman’ (see Jonker ).
Americanized form of various cognate or like-sounding names in other languages, notably German   Junger   and   Junker  , or Dutch   Jonker  .

variants.name: Ogg
meanings: Scottish: Anglicized form of a nickname from the Gaelic adjective óg ‘young’, used to distinguish the junior of two bearers of the same personal name (typically, father and son).
English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Hogg  , pronounced without initial /h/.

variants.name: Younge
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Fitzroy, Sinead.

English: variant of   Young  . — Note: The population figure published by the US Census Bureau for this surname in the year 2000 is as high as 823, while the figure for 2010 is supposedly lower than 100.

nameMeanings.name: Nancy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of uncertain origin. From the 18th century it is clearly used as a pet form of   Ann   (  see  Nan  ), but it may originally have been a similar formation deriving from the common medieval given name   Annis  , a vernacular form of   Agnes .  Nowadays it is an independent name, and was especially popular in America in the 1930s, 40s, and 50s.

variants.name: Nancie
meanings: Of uncertain origin. From the 18th century it is clearly used as a pet form of   Ann   (  see  Nan  ), but it may originally have been a similar formation deriving from the common medieval given name   Annis  , a vernacular form of   Agnes .  Nowadays it is an independent name, and was especially popular in America in the 1930s, 40s, and 50s.

variants.name: Nanci
meanings: Of uncertain origin. From the 18th century it is clearly used as a pet form of   Ann   (  see  Nan  ), but it may originally have been a similar formation deriving from the common medieval given name   Annis  , a vernacular form of   Agnes .  Nowadays it is an independent name, and was especially popular in America in the 1930s, 40s, and 50s.

historicEvents.title: Movement to Washington D.C.
historicEvents.description: While the growth of the new nation was exponential, the United States didn’t have permanent location to house the Government. The First capital was temporary in New York City but by the second term of George Washington the Capital moved to Philadelphia for the following 10 years. Ultimately during the Presidency of John Adams, the Capital found a permanent home in the District of Columbia.
France sells Louisiana territories to U.S.A.

historicEvents.title: Alabama Becomes a State
historicEvents.description: Alabama became the twenty-second state admitted to the Union on December 14, 1819.

historicEvents.title: Slave Rebellion
historicEvents.description: "On June 16, 1822,  Denmark Vesey a free and self-educated African American leads a slave rebellion called ""the rising."" The interesting thing about this rebellion is that it does not really happen. The only thing the judges have to go on is the testimony of people that witness it."

historicEvents.title: Orders No. 25 Removes Cherokees
historicEvents.description: A small group of Cherokees from Georgia voluntarily migrated to the Indian Territory. The remaining Cherokees in Georgia resisted the mounting pressure to leave. In 1838, U.S. President Martin Van Buren ordered U.S. troops to remove the Cherokee Nation. The troops gathered the Cherokees and marched them and other Native Americans from North Carolina, Tennessee, and Alabama along what is now known as “The Trail of Tears.” Approximately 5,000 Cherokees died on their way to Indian Territory.
Historical Boundaries: 1850: Gordon, Georgia, United States
In 1860, South Carolina quit the United States because its citizens were in favor of slavery and President Lincoln was not. The Civil War started a year later.

Civil War History - Some 11,000 Georgians gave their lives in defense of their state a state that suffered immense destruction. But wars end brought about an even more dramatic figure to tell: 460,000 African-Americans were set free from the shackles of slavery to begin new lives as free people.

en.lifeSummary: When Nancy Young was born about 1800, in South Carolina, United States, her father, Isaac Young IV, was 42 and her mother, MARY Odingsell, was 42. She had at least 2 sons and 4 daughters with John Allen Griffith. She lived in Gordon, Georgia, United States in 1850 and Eastern Division, Walker, Alabama, United States in 1860. She died before 18 June 1870, in Alabama, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Nancy Young est née environ 1800, à Caroline du Sud, États-Unis, son père, Isaac Young IV, avait 42 ans et sa mère, MARY Odingsell, avait 42 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles avec John Allen Griffith. Elle a vécu à Gordon, Géorgie, États-Unis en 1850 et à Eastern Division, Walker, Alabama, États-Unis en 1860. Elle est décédée avant 18 juin 1870, à Alabama, États-Unis.

Sie hatte mit John Allen Griffith mindestens 2 Söhne und 4 Töchter. 1850 hat sie in Gordon, Georgia, Vereinigte Staaten und 1860 in Eastern Division, Walker, Alabama, Vereinigte Staaten gelebt. Sie starb vor 18. Juni 1870 in Alabama, Vereinigte Staaten.

ja.lifeSummary: Nancy Youngが約1800年にアメリカ合衆国サウスカロライナ州で生まれた時，父Isaac Young IVは42歳，母MARY Odingsellは42歳であった。彼女はJohn Allen Griffithとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と4人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1850年にはアメリカ合衆国ジョージア州ゴルドンに，1860年にはアメリカ合衆国アラバマ州ウォーカーエアステルン ディヴィシオンに住んでいた。彼女は1870年6月18日以前にアメリカ合衆国アラバマ州で死去した。

ko.Eastern Division, Marshall, Alabama, Uni: 미국 앨라배마 주 워커 이스터 디비시언
ko.lifeSummary: Nancy Young(이)가 대략 1800년 미국 사우스캐롤라이나 주에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Isaac Young IV(은)는 42세였고, 그녀의 어머니 MARY Odingsell(은)는 42세였다. 그녀는 John Allen Griffith(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 1850년도에 미국 조지아 주 고든에서, 1860년도에 미국 앨라배마 주 워커 이스터 디비시언에서 살았다. 그녀는 1870년 6월 18일 이전 미국 앨라배마 주에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Nancy Young родилась около 1800 г. в местности Южная Каролина, Соединенные штаты, её отцу по имени Isaac Young IV было 42 года, а матери, которую звали MARY Odingsell, 42. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери от John Allen Griffith. Она жила в местности гордон, Джорджия, Соединенные штаты в 1850 году и в местности эастирн дивисион, валкир, Алабама, Соединенные штаты в 1860 году. Она умерла перед 18 июнь 1870 г. в местности Алабама, Соединенные штаты.
